agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A feature request proposes adding a 'confirmed' status to the Quotes bounded context. The term 'confirmed' is already established in the Orders bounded context with a precise, different meaning. This is a ubiquitous language collision that, if shipped, will corrupt domain communication across the organization.","bounded_context":"Quotes (proposed change) vs Orders (existing definition)","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Confirmed Order","definition":"ESTABLISHED: An order that has been paid and is queued for fulfillment. It is immutable — no further changes are permitted."},{"term":"Confirmed Quote (PROPOSED)","definition":"PROPOSED: A quote where the customer has verbally agreed to terms but payment has NOT occurred. The quote is still mutable (payment terms, final adjustments may still apply)."},{"term":"Quote","definition":"A pre-order artifact representing proposed terms, pricing, and line items offered to a customer."},{"term":"Order","definition":"A transactional artifact representing a committed purchase that progresses through fulfillment."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A 'confirmed' order is immutable — no field may be modified after confirmation.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Confirmed orders are financial commitments backed by payment. Mutability would break audit trails, accounting integrity, and fulfillment reliability.","testable_assertion":"Given an order in 'confirmed' status, any attempt to modify any field must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"A 'confirmed' order has been paid. Payment is a precondition of order confirmation.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Payment is the event that transitions an order to confirmed. Without payment, the order is not confirmed — this is a state integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Given an order, it can only transition to 'confirmed' if a successful payment event has been recorded."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"The proposed 'confirmed quote' has NOT been paid and is NOT immutable — it represents verbal agreement only.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a proposed business policy for quote lifecycle management. It could be adjusted (e.g., requiring written agreement instead of verbal).","testable_assertion":"Given a quote in the proposed 'confirmed' status, no payment record exists and the quote may still be subject to modification before order creation."}],"acceptance_criteria":[],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"A developer reads 'confirmed' in the Quotes context and assumes immutability (as in Orders), accidentally preventing legitimate quote modifications before payment.","expected_behavior":"The system must NOT enforce immutability on confirmed quotes, but the shared term makes this mistake highly likely."},{"scenario":"A support agent sees a 'confirmed' item in the system and assumes payment has occurred, telling the customer their order is being fulfilled.","expected_behavior":"The agent should understand no payment has occurred, but the overloaded term will cause confusion in cross-context communication."},{"scenario":"Reporting dashboards aggregate 'confirmed' items across quotes and orders, conflating unpaid verbal agreements with paid immutable orders.","expected_behavior":"Reports must distinguish between the two, but shared terminology makes silent conflation inevitable."},{"scenario":"A verbal agreement (confirmed quote) is later disputed by the customer, but staff treat it as binding because 'confirmed' implies finality in the Orders context.","expected_behavior":"The quote should be cancellable or modifiable since no payment has occurred, but the term signals otherwise."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"High risk of revenue misreporting. If 'confirmed quotes' (unpaid) are conflated with 'confirmed orders' (paid) in dashboards or accounting, the business could overstate committed revenue. This has compliance implications.","operational":"Significant cross-team confusion. Sales, fulfillment, support, and engineering will use 'confirmed' to mean two contradictory things: paid+immutable vs unpaid+mutable. Every handoff between Quotes and Orders becomes an error-prone translation.","user":"Sales agents may understand 'confirmed quote' intuitively, but downstream teams (fulfillment, finance, support) will misinterpret the status, leading to premature fulfillment attempts or incorrect customer communications.","risk":"Domain language corruption is one of the highest-risk forms of technical debt. It compounds silently — every new team member, every integration, every report inherits the ambiguity. The cost to fix grows exponentially over time.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Sales Agents","impact":"Positive — they get a status reflecting verbal agreement, which matches their workflow."},{"group":"Fulfillment Team","impact":"Negative — 'confirmed' signals readiness for fulfillment in their context. A confirmed quote is NOT ready for fulfillment. Confusion is certain."},{"group":"Finance / Accounting","impact":"Negative — 'confirmed' implies payment received. Confirmed quotes have no payment. Risk of revenue misstatement."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Negative — agents will tell customers their 'confirmed' item is paid and in progress when it is not."},{"group":"Engineering","impact":"Negative — developers working across bounded contexts will apply wrong invariants (e.g., immutability) to the wrong entity."}]},"confidence":{"level":45,"reasoning":"Confidence is low because the feature request contains a direct contradiction with established domain language. The term 'confirmed' has a precise, invariant-bearing meaning in the Orders context (paid + immutable), and the proposal redefines it in the Quotes context to mean the opposite (unpaid + mutable). This is not a matter of interpretation — it is a semantic collision that must be resolved before acceptance criteria can be defined.","high_confidence_areas":["The existing definition of 'confirmed order' is clear and invariant-bearing","The proposed 'confirmed quote' contradicts the established meaning on two axes: payment status and mutability","This will cause cross-context confusion"],"low_confidence_areas":["What the business actually wants to call this quote status (needs renaming)","Whether verbal agreement carries any binding business rules beyond status tracking","Whether there are other quote statuses in the lifecycle that need to be considered"],"assumptions":["The Orders bounded context definition is authoritative and in active use","The Quotes and Orders contexts share communication channels (dashboards, support tools, APIs) where term collision will manifest"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"The term 'confirmed' is already defined in the Orders context as 'paid and immutable.' The feature request proposes using 'confirmed' in the Quotes context to mean 'verbally agreed, unpaid, and mutable.' These definitions are mutually exclusive on two critical axes (payment status and mutability). Shipping this overloaded term will corrupt the ubiquitous language across bounded contexts.","affected_stakeholders":["Sales Agents","Fulfillment Team","Finance / Accounting","Customer Support","Engineering"],"options":["A: Reject the term 'confirmed' for quotes. Use a distinct term such as 'accepted,' 'agreed,' or 'verbally-committed' that accurately conveys the quote's state without colliding with the Orders context.","B: Accept the overloaded term and rely on context (quote vs order) to disambiguate — accepting the operational risk of cross-context confusion.","C: Rename the existing order status to something more specific (e.g., 'paid-confirmed') and allow 'confirmed' for quotes — high migration cost, breaks existing integrations."],"recommendation":"Option A. The Quotes context should adopt a term like 'accepted' or 'verbally-committed.' This preserves the integrity of 'confirmed' in the Orders context, avoids cross-team confusion, and costs nothing to implement since the feature is new. The Orders definition is load-bearing and invariant — changing it (Option C) is far more expensive and risky than naming the new status correctly from the start."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Ubiquitous language collision: 'confirmed' is redefined with contradictory semantics across bounded contexts.","business_rule_broken":"BR-1 and BR-2 — the established invariants of 'confirmed' (immutability and payment-required) are contradicted by the proposed quote status, which is mutable and unpaid."}]}
